name: SidebarMenuButtonProps
description:
The interactive element inside a `SidebarMenuItem`. Renders a button, or a link
when `href` is provided.

isActive.description: Marks the item as the active page (highlighted, and `aria-current` on links).

tooltip.description: Label shown in a tooltip when the sidebar is collapsed to icons.

href.description: Render as a link to this destination instead of a button.

render.type: ((props: DetailedHTMLProps<HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, HTMLSpanElement> | DetailedHTMLProps<LinkWithRequiredHref, HTMLAnchorElement>, renderProps: LinkRenderProps) => ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>>)
render.detailedType:
| ((
    props:
      | DetailedHTMLProps<
          HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>,
          HTMLSpanElement
        >
      | DetailedHTMLProps<
          LinkWithRequiredHref,
          HTMLAnchorElement
        >,
    renderProps: LinkRenderProps,
  ) => ReactElement<
    unknown,
    string | JSXElementConstructor<any>
  >)
| undefined
render.description: Escape hatch to render the link with a custom element (e.g. a router link).

onPress.description: Handler that is called when the press is released over the target.

onPressStart.description: Handler that is called when a press interaction starts.

onPressEnd.description:
Handler that is called when a press interaction ends, either
over the target or when the pointer leaves the target.

onPressChange.description: Handler that is called when the press state changes.

onPressUp.description:
Handler that is called when a press is released over the target, regardless of
whether it started on the target or not.

onClick.description:
**Not recommended – use `onPress` instead.** `onClick` is an alias for `onPress`
provided for compatibility with other libraries. `onPress` provides
additional event details for non-mouse interactions.

onFocus.description: Handler that is called when the element receives focus.

onBlur.description: Handler that is called when the element loses focus.

onFocusChange.description: Handler that is called when the element's focus status changes.

onKeyDown.description: Handler that is called when a key is pressed.

onKeyUp.description: Handler that is called when a key is released.

onHoverStart.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction starts.

onHoverEnd.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction ends.

onHoverChange.description: Handler that is called when the hover state changes.

aria-controls.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current
element.

aria-current.description:
Indicates whether this element represents the current item within a container or set of related
elements.

aria-describedby.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

aria-details.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) that provide a detailed, extended description for the
object.

aria-disabled.description: Indicates whether the element is disabled to users of assistive technology.

aria-expanded.description:
Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded
or collapsed.

aria-haspopup.description:
Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that
can be triggered by an element.

aria-label.description: Defines a string value that labels the current element.

aria-labelledby.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

aria-pressed.description: Indicates the current "pressed" state of toggle buttons.

autoFocus.description: Whether the element should receive focus on render.

excludeFromTabOrder.description:
Whether to exclude the element from the sequential tab order. If true,
the element will not be focusable via the keyboard by tabbing. This should
be avoided except in rare scenarios where an alternative means of accessing
the element or its functionality via the keyboard is available.

form.description:
The `<form>` element to associate the button with.
The value of this attribute must be the id of a `<form>` in the same document.
See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button#form).

formAction.description:
The URL that processes the information submitted by the button.
Overrides the action attribute of the button's form owner.

formEncType.description: Indicates how to encode the form data that is submitted.

formMethod.description: Indicates the HTTP method used to submit the form.

formNoValidate.description: Indicates that the form is not to be validated when it is submitted.

formTarget.description: Overrides the target attribute of the button's form owner.

isPending.description:
Whether the button is in a pending state. This disables press and hover events
while retaining focusability, and announces the pending state to screen readers.

name.description: Submitted as a pair with the button's value as part of the form data.

preventFocusOnPress.description:
Whether to prevent focus from moving to the button when pressing it.

Caution, this can make the button inaccessible and should only be used when alternative
keyboard interaction is provided, such as ComboBox's MenuTrigger or a NumberField's
increment/decrement control.

slot.description:
A slot name for the component. Slots allow the component to receive props from a parent
component. An explicit `null` value indicates that the local props completely override all
props received from a parent.

style.description:
The inline [style](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/style) for the
element. A function may be provided to compute the style based on component state.

type.description: The behavior of the button when used in an HTML form.

value.description: The value associated with the button's name when it's submitted with the form data.

elements.name: new (props: any, context: any) => Component<any, any, any>

isFocused.name: isFocused
isFocused.description: Whether the button is focused, either via a mouse or keyboard.

isFocusVisible.name: isFocusVisible
isFocusVisible.description: Whether the button is keyboard focused.

isHovered.name: isHovered
isHovered.description: Whether the button is currently hovered with a mouse.

isPending.name: isPending
isPending.description: Whether the button is currently in a pending state.

isPressed.name: isPressed
isPressed.description: Whether the button is currently in a pressed state.

pointerType.name: pointerType
pointerType.description: The pointer type that triggered the hover event.

target.name: target
target.description: The target element of the hover event.

type.name: type
type.description: The type of hover event being fired.

altKey.name: altKey
altKey.description: Whether the alt keyboard modifier was held during the press event.

continuePropagation.name: continuePropagation
continuePropagation.description:
By default, press events stop propagation to parent elements.
In cases where a handler decides not to handle a specific event,
it can call `continuePropagation()` to allow a parent to handle it.

ctrlKey.name: ctrlKey
ctrlKey.description: Whether the ctrl keyboard modifier was held during the press event.

key.name: key
key.description:
The key that triggered the press event, if it was triggered by a keyboard interaction.
This is useful for differentiating between Space and Enter key presses.

metaKey.name: metaKey
metaKey.description: Whether the meta keyboard modifier was held during the press event.

pointerType.name: pointerType
pointerType.description: The pointer type that triggered the press event.

shiftKey.name: shiftKey
shiftKey.description: Whether the shift keyboard modifier was held during the press event.

target.name: target
target.description: The target element of the press event.

type.name: type
type.description: The type of press event being fired.

x.name: x
x.description: X position relative to the target.

y.name: y
y.description: Y position relative to the target.

isCurrent.name: isCurrent
isCurrent.description: Whether the link is the current item within a list.

isFocused.name: isFocused
isFocused.description: Whether the link is focused, either via a mouse or keyboard.

isFocusVisible.name: isFocusVisible
isFocusVisible.description: Whether the link is keyboard focused.

isHovered.name: isHovered
isHovered.description: Whether the link is currently hovered with a mouse.

isPressed.name: isPressed
isPressed.description: Whether the link is currently in a pressed state.

return.name: {} & { readonly content: (slotProps?: ({} & ClassProp<ClassNameValue>) | undefined) => string; readonly arrow: (slotProps?: ({} & ClassProp<ClassNameValue>) | undefined) => string; } & {}

typeParameters.name: { readonly content: readonly ["w-fit max-w-xs origin-(--trigger-anchor-point) rounded-(--tooltip-radius) bg-tooltip px-3 py-1.5 text-center text-xs text-fg-on-tooltip shadow-[var(--shadow-overlay,none)] forced-color-adjust-none outline-none", "transition-[transform,opacity,scale] duration-200 ease-out will-change-[t...

aria-describedby.name: aria-describedby
aria-describedby.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

aria-details.name: aria-details
aria-details.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) that provide a detailed, extended description for the
object.

aria-label.name: aria-label
aria-label.description: Defines a string value that labels the current element.

aria-labelledby.name: aria-labelledby
aria-labelledby.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

arrowBoundaryOffset.name: arrowBoundaryOffset
arrowBoundaryOffset.description: The minimum distance the arrow's edge should be from the edge of the overlay element.

children.name: children
children.description:
The children of the component. A function may be provided to alter the children based on
component state.

className.name: className
className.description:
The CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the
element. A function may be provided to compute the class based on component state.

containerPadding.name: containerPadding
containerPadding.description:
The placement padding that should be applied between the element and its
surrounding container.

crossOffset.name: crossOffset
crossOffset.description:
The additional offset applied along the cross axis between the element and its
anchor element.

defaultOpen.name: defaultOpen
defaultOpen.description: Whether the overlay is open by default (uncontrolled).

isEntering.name: isEntering
isEntering.description: Whether the tooltip is currently performing an entry animation.

isExiting.name: isExiting
isExiting.description: Whether the tooltip is currently performing an exit animation.

offset.name: offset
offset.description:
The additional offset applied along the main axis between the element and its
anchor element.

onOpenChange.name: onOpenChange
onOpenChange.description: Handler that is called when the overlay's open state changes.

placement.name: placement
placement.description: The placement of the tooltip with respect to the trigger.

ref.name: ref
ref.description:
Allows getting a ref to the component instance.
Once the component unmounts, React will set `ref.current` to `null`
(or call the ref with `null` if you passed a callback ref).

render.name: render
render.description:
Overrides the default DOM element with a custom render function.
This allows rendering existing components with built-in styles and behaviors
such as router links, animation libraries, and pre-styled components.

Requirements:

- You must render the expected element type (e.g. if `<button>` is expected, you cannot render an
  `<a>`).
- Only a single root DOM element can be rendered (no fragments).
- You must pass through props and ref to the underlying DOM element, merging with your own prop
  as appropriate.

shouldFlip.name: shouldFlip
shouldFlip.description:
Whether the element should flip its orientation (e.g. top to bottom or left to right) when
there is insufficient room for it to render completely.

style.name: style
style.description:
The inline [style](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/style) for the
element. A function may be provided to compute the style based on component state.

triggerRef.name: triggerRef
triggerRef.description:
The ref for the element which the tooltip positions itself with respect to.

When used within a TooltipTrigger this is set automatically. It is only required when used
standalone.

UNSTABLE_portalContainer.name: UNSTABLE_portalContainer
UNSTABLE_portalContainer.description:
The container element in which the overlay portal will be placed. This may have unknown
behavior depending on where it is portalled to.
